# Verity Mod Download Guide Full Context

VerityModChecker.online helps Minecraft players find the current Verity Mod download route without using a random mirror. The site focuses on current public project status, Java versus Bedrock separation, MCPE setup, VERITY.exe modpack confusion, AI model setup, voice troubleshooting, and 404 or taken-down links.

The homepage leads with a current Java and Bedrock file map checked July 19, 2026. It links directly to exact maintainer release records, identifies owners, Project IDs, filenames, file sizes, publication dates, and point-in-time project reach. The same facts are available as machine-readable JSON and an Atom update feed. The source and file checker reviews URLs, Project IDs, release record numbers, filenames, package extensions, and displayed file sizes. It calculates local SHA-256 fingerprints without uploading a selected file. The route chooser separates Java, Bedrock, and MCPE setup questions.

The verified current records are: Verity JE Forge file `verity-5.7.2.jar`, record `8450350`, 235.06 MB, for Minecraft 1.20.1; Verity BE file `Verity (Stable) (1.0.8)-(26.3).mcaddon`, record `8420981`, 18.80 MB, for Bedrock 26.30; and PnTMC alternate file `ThatMob's Verity 2.1.0 by PnTMC [Add-on] - V26.30.mcaddon`, record `8327253`, 35.45 MB, for Bedrock 26.30. Metadata matching can establish that a route or filename corresponds to the recorded public release, but it is not a malware verdict.

The what-is-verity-mod page answers the rising searches "what is Verity Mod," "what is Verity Minecraft," and "is Verity real." It separates the ThatMob character and produced horror videos from public community software. It also identifies the current Java and Bedrock project records without claiming that every recreation is the original video build.

The how-to-get-verity-mod page answers download and installation intent. It starts with edition selection, gives a clean-profile Java order, gives a fresh-world Bedrock order, and delays Groq, Ollama, voice, commands, and experimental features until the base project loads.

The download page is the main status tracker. It explains that Verity JE, Verity BE, Verity - Bedrock Edition, and VERITY.exe answer overlapping searches but are different routes. Java jars do not import into Bedrock. Bedrock .mcaddon files do not belong in the Java mods folder. A modpack is a profile route and should not be treated as the same artifact as a standalone Java jar.

The what-happened page answers searches like "what happened to Verity Mod" and "did Verity Mod get taken down." The page explains that some old video links, old project pages, and comments can point to removed or stale files, while current public routes still exist. It tells players to rebuild the route by project name, edition, version, and file type instead of trusting the first mirror.

The Verity JE page is for exact Java intent. It covers Forge 1.20.1, NeoForge 1.21.1, Groq, Ollama, voice, clean profile testing, loader matching, and dependency order. It tells players to debug Minecraft version and loader before debugging AI.

The Bedrock and MCPE pages cover addon import, behavior pack activation, resource pack activation, Beta APIs, commands, mobile restart, clean test worlds, and why Java instructions do not apply to Bedrock.

The VERITY.exe page explains that VERITY.exe is a CurseForge modpack route. It may be relevant when a player names the modpack directly, but it is not the same as Verity JE and not the same as a Bedrock addon.

The AI model page explains that Groq is the hosted route and Ollama is the local route. The page does not collect keys. It warns players not to paste API keys into download pages, Reddit, Discord, or random forms. It also separates text response problems from voice output problems.

The not-working, voice-not-working, and taken-down pages handle symptom searches: crashes, old links, 404 pages, unsafe mirrors, box not spawning, dialogue missing, only saying "...", chat works but voice is silent, and suspected unsafe reposts.

VerityModChecker.online is fan-made and independent. It is not affiliated with Mojang, Microsoft, CurseForge, Groq, Ollama, ThatMob, or Verity maintainers. It does not host mod files, does not collect API keys, and does not claim ownership of Verity-related projects.
